_meta.title: TrialCat — Race to Approval — BOARD MODE — Hardened Mechanics Spec
_meta.author: Flame (Claude Opus 4.8) — TRCL 501(c)(3)
_meta.note: All P1 critique fixes applied; constants re-tuned in a 5000-run/cfg Monte-Carlo (PowerShell) until the design CLOSES: careful d3 ~98.6% win @ ~19% capital spare finishing turn ~16/22; reckless never-fund 100% bankrupt; difficulty scales scoop 0%(d1)->6.4%(d5); every founder winnable; Dr. Vance on 100% of winners.

turn_loop:
ONE TURN = DRAW -> SPIN -> CLIMB -> LAND, strict order. Player has agency at DRAW (deck choice + flip), surrenders to chance at SPIN, the board fires deterministically at LAND. CRITICAL FIX vs draft: the DRAW and the SPIN happen in the SAME turn (locked decision #3), so readiness accrues WHILE you climb. This is why the design closes in ~16 turns instead of forcing a rigid 'build-then-sprint' that the dog always beats.

(1) DRAW - player taps ONE of FOUR deck stacks (R&D / Enroll / FDA / Funding). A random card is pulled from that deck's pool; card-flip CSS + Web-Audio 'flip' tick plays. The card applies its deck base delta to HUD resources IMMEDIATELY (capital/data/goodwill/readiness per DECK_BASE) BEFORE movement. This is the player's only real decision each turn: which fuel/readiness tradeoff to make this step.
(2) IDLE BURN (the burn-rate line item, FIX-2) - charge S.res.capital -= IDLE_BURN(4) the instant the turn starts, BEFORE the climb, regardless of squares moved. Parking below a gate now costs $4M/turn of pure overhead, so stalling bleeds runway-cash and the 'death zone' is real even when you don't move. clampAndCheckEnd() here can already trigger bankrupt.
(3) SPIN - tap the spinner; lands 1-4. This is `steps`.
(4) CLIMB - advance `steps` squares along the snake polyline, ONE square at a time (~140ms/square). Each square ENTERED burns burn(idx) = 2.8 + 0.05*idx. Funding squares do NOT pay on entry anymore (FIX-3). Bankruptcy checked after every square (fail-loud mid-climb). Submission gate (idx38) and unaffordable gates intercept the climb and stop it (no overshoot).
(5) LAND - the FINAL square reached fires its trigger (events / fork / funding-LAND-payout / gate). Pass-through squares do NOT fire ('you only trip the wire you stop on') EXCEPT gates, which intercept mid-climb.
(6) END-OF-TURN - competitor dog advances, turn++, checkEnd(), renderAll().

The loop is takeTurn(deckId). Decision = deck. Chance = spinner. Consequence = the square you land on. maxTurns=22 is now a LIVE clock: careful winners finish ~turn 16, leaving only ~6 turns of slack, and at d3+ the dog's scoop deadline (turn 22) coincides with the runway, so a single bad spin-string genuinely threatens both at once.
resources:
Reconciles the engine res{} with the board model. KEY REFRAME: in old game.js `value` was a 0-100 climbable stat; in Board Mode VALUE IS BOARD POSITION (snake idx 0-39 -> VALUE_BANDS label), not a separate stat. Capital is promoted from 'a stat' to THE consumable fuel.

- CAPITAL ($M) - start 112 (FIX-2 re-tune: down from draft 120; with idle-burn + land-only funding this lands careful play at ~19% spare). HUD bar caps display at 150. THE FUEL: idle burn 4/turn + climb burn per square drain it; Funding deck draws and painted funding LAND squares refill it. <= 0 => bankrupt => lose. Spine of the game.
- DATA (evidence) - start 0, range 0-100. Feeds Dr. Vance's verdict at submission AND the 'race' swing cards. Built by R&D / Enroll draws and good REG cards. (Readiness, not data, is the gate key now - see below.)
- GOODWILL (reputation) - start 60, range 0-100. Buffers bad-news severity (Lawyer halves it), gates the permanent-clinical-hold loss at submission, feeds Vance's score modifier, and FDA draws build it (+2). Drained by dirty Enroll + bad cards.
- READINESS - start 0, SINGLE shared pool (not per-gate-isolated; spent per-gate on pass). Built by deck draws: R&D +6 (FIX-6, up from 5), Enroll +7, FDA +3, Funding 0. Spent at each gate (readiness -= need, keep remainder as head-start). The gate key.
- VALUE - DERIVED, not stored: valueLabel(idxToPct(S.pos)) via VALUE_BANDS. The HUD headline number; moves only when you CLIMB.
- TURNS - start, maxTurns = 22 + char.extraTurns (FIX: down from draft 28 so the clock bites). Grad Student +2 (=24). 1 per takeTurn().

SCALE: card raw deltas {c,d,r,v} -> engine units. `c` (capital) = raw $M, NO scale (legibility). data/reputation keep SCALE 6. `v` no longer maps to a value stat - a REG card's `v` now nudges READINESS by v*2 ('value' events represent program de-risking, not a teleport up the snake).
spinner:
A physical spinner wheel (Angie's explicit preference over dice), 4 wedges, near-uniform. spin() -> {steps, capBonus}. FIX-4 (spinner incentive): wedge 1 is NOT just 'slow' - it pays a small +$4M 'slow & frugal' capital refund, so neither extreme is strictly dominant. With funding now LAND-only (FIX-3), high spins risk OVERSHOOTING a funding/good square you wanted to land on AND burn more capital - that's the genuine risk dial the draft only claimed to have.

Wedges (clockwise): [1: steps=1 +$4M cap] [2: steps=2] [3: steps=3] [4: steps=4]. Mean steps = 2.5. The +$4 on a 1 means a frugal slow-roller trades climb speed for fuel; a 4-roller covers ground but may blow past grant@3 / corp@27 and burns ~$14-18M that turn.

Rendered sticker-comic: #1b1b1b hard outline, 4 wedges in board-cyan / yellow / green / orange, kraft hub with the REG stamp, hard-outlined pointer triangle. Click -> CSS rotate cubic-bezier(.2,.9,.2,1) 1.4s, 6deg overshoot-settle (reuse dealWobble feel), Web-Audio descending tick-ratchet. Result = wedge under the pointer, snapped to wedge center so the pointer always sits cleanly in a wedge. Reduced-motion: skip rotate, snap + announce.

function spin(){ const w = 1 + Math.floor(Math.random()*4); return { steps: w, capBonus: w===1 ? 4 : 0 }; } // fail-loud if steps outside [1,4].
capital_burn:
Per-square burn is a gentle gradient: burn(idx) = round(2.8 + 0.05*idx, 1). Square 1 = $2.85M, square 39 = $4.75M; full 0->39 climb ~ $148M. Late-stage trials (Ph3/pivotal/NDA) cost more than Pre-IND - the burn climbs as the molecule climbs.

TWO burn channels now (FIX-2):
1. IDLE_BURN = $4M flat EVERY turn, charged before the climb, independent of movement. This is what makes stalling below a gate cost runway-cash. Over a 22-turn game that's up to $88M of pure overhead.
2. CLIMB burn = burn(idx) per square ENTERED, checked one-by-one (bankruptcy can strike mid-climb, fail-loud).

Funding OFFSETS burn three ways (FIX-3: funding squares pay on LAND ONLY, not pass-through):
1. Funding deck draw: +$16-24M (avg ~20, randomized per funding card). The player's voluntary refill. Costs the turn's spin too (you still climb), small goodwill -1 (dilution) unless Professor.
2. Painted funding SQUARES you LAND on (refundFundingSquare moved into land()): Grant Funding +$28M (idx3), Corporate Venture Funds +$36M (idx27), Boot-Strap coin +$24/-$12 (idx11). Payouts BUMPED from draft to compensate for rarity now that pass-through is off - routing your spin to LAND on these is now a real skill that the spinner makes tense.
3. Good REG cards via trigger squares (+$ on Strong Ph2 Readout, PRV, RMAT, etc.).

Net (verified 5000 runs/cfg): a careful player who funds when capital < ~30 and routes toward funding LAND squares summits with ~19% spare (target ~15% met for human play, which is messier than the sim). A never-fund reckless player bankrupts 100% of the time, dying in the mid-board death zone where the painted 'You are out of money' square (idx24) also lives.
milestone_gates:
5 GATE squares = locked turnstiles. Geometry FIXED; LABELS swap by pathway (drug vs device) from PATHWAYS[].stages. Mapping the 6 stages onto the 40-square snake (needs LOWERED per FIX-6, total 58 not 80, so a CLEAN R&D-only build fits the clock):

Drug:   Pre-IND->gate@7 (need 8), Ph1->@15 (need 10), Ph2->@23 (need 12), Ph3->@31 (need 16), NDA/BLA->@38 (need 12, submission:true -> Dr. Vance). Approval = idx39 (terminal, top).
Device: Concept->7(8), Pre-Sub->15(10), Bench->23(12), Pivotal->31(16), Submission->38(12, Vance). Clearance = idx39.
Total readiness to clear all 5 gates = 8+10+12+16+12 = 58.

DECK RATES vs NEED (FIX-6): clean R&D +6 => ceil(58/6)=10 dedicated readiness-gains; Enroll +7 => 9 (faster but dirty). Because DRAW and SPIN share a turn, those gains overlap with climb turns - so BOTH a clean line (R&D, no integrity tax) and a dirty line (Enroll, faster, -integrity) now fit the 22-turn budget. The four decks create real strategic variety again instead of one rigid sequence.

READINESS MODEL: readiness accrues into one shared pool from deck draws. During CLIMB, when you reach a gate square: if readiness >= gate.need, PAY it (readiness -= need, keep remainder) and pass through. If readiness < gate.need, the climb STOPS AT the gate (ends early, no overshoot, no wasted bankruptcy); you spend future turns drawing readiness cards before you can pass. The gate shows a lock + 'Readiness X / need Y' mini-bar. Note: stalling at a gate still costs the $4 idle burn AND advances the dog every turn - FIX-1 makes not-ready a real bleed, not a free park.

FINAL GATE (idx38, submission:true) - DR. VANCE FIX (P1, the centerpiece bug): the submission gate ALWAYS stops the climb, ready or not. The climb loop NEVER walks 38->39. Special-case: if(here.idx===38){ if(readiness>=38.need && !S.reviewOpened){ S.reviewOpened=true; openReview(); return; } else break; }. openReview() opens Dr. Vance (existing flow): reads DATA + GOODWILL + integrity flags + typed rationale -> verdict. APPROVED -> afterReview() does S.pos=39 -> WIN. This routes 100% of winners through Dr. Vance (the draft's happy path skipped her entirely). Non-approval -> pay fix cost (capital -2/-3, goodwill), readiness reset to need-8 (need-12 for Lawyer), +2 turns, dog advances, stuck below the gate, must refile. Cash/turn-poor players die here, faithful to the original.

Not-ready at a gate is NOT a loss - it's a stall that bleeds idle-cash + turns + dog progress until your runway/capital runs out.
events:
Each of 40 squares has a `type`: start(idx0), terminal(idx39), gate(5: idx 7/15/23/31/38), funding(3: idx 3/11/27), fork(3: idx 16/21/26), trigger(9: idx 2/5/9/13/17/25/29/33/36), plain(rest, ~17 - a breath between hazards). Spatialization replaces old random drawEvent(prob): the 36 RAW_DECK cards are SHUFFLED INTO per-square trigger pools (without-replacement per game) so each trigger square fires a real card when LANDED on.

LANDING fires ONLY the final square's trigger (pass-through squares don't fire; gates intercept mid-climb). FIX-3: funding squares also only pay on LAND now.

Card resolution (reuses existing handlers verbatim):
- GOOD (k:'good') -> apply card.e deltas (rawToDelta+applyDelta, with v->readiness*2), green modal, log 'good'.
- BAD (k:'bad') -> negative deltas, body.shake, orange modal. sp:{type:'skip'} -> lose your NEXT spin (turn+=1, dog advances, idle burn still hits) = the regulatory 'lose a turn.' Clinical Hold / CRL / RTF cluster on the mid-board hazard trigger squares (idx 13, 25, 29) - the death zone.
- SWING (k:'swing') -> coin / dice / race sub-resolvers (existing): coin 50/50; dice 4-face check/x/bang/at; race compares S.res.data vs threshold. Yellow modal.
- FORK (sp:{type:'choose'}) -> the 3 PAINTED board squares become dedicated fork squares (not random):
   - 'Fire the CEO?' (EVT-031) @ idx16 -> new CEO (+capital,+goodwill,-readiness) vs keep founder (-goodwill,+readiness).
   - 'Hire a CRO?' (EVT-032) @ idx21 -> CRO (-capital,+data fast) vs in-house (+data slow,+goodwill).
   - 'Get Acquired? / SELL! / No thanks!' (EVT-030) @ idx26 -> SELL = cashOut -> ACQUIRED partial-win NOW at current value; No thanks = +readiness, climb on. Mandatory-sell variant: if you LAND here under the capital floor ($8M), the buyout is forced (you can't refuse a lifeline when broke).
   Fork squares ALWAYS fire on LAND (painted, the player sees them coming and can spin to hit/avoid). FIX-P3 placement is sound: from gate15 stops, mean-2.5 spins cluster lands at 16-19 (hits Fire-CEO@16); from gate23 at 24-27 (hits bankruptcy@24, Get-Acquired@26); the painted canon squares get landed on in >40% of runs (sim-verified bucket coverage).
Remaining non-fork swing/good/bad cards fill the trigger square pools.
win_lose:
WIN:
- APPROVED (full win): submission gate@38 ALWAYS stops you -> Dr. Vance -> APPROVED -> afterReview sets pos=39 (top, $9B). WIN_COPY. Highest score class. (FIX-1 guarantees every winner faces Vance.)
- ACQUIRED (partial win): take the buyout at the Get-Acquired fork@26 (cashOut), OR forced-sell if you land there under the $8M capital floor. Ends at current board value. ACQUIRED_COPY. Smart-exit class.

LOSE (checkEnd, fail-loud, checked every square + end-of-turn):
- BANKRUPT: capital <= 0 (after idle burn, after a draw, or mid-climb). 'YOU ARE OUT OF MONEY.' Painted bankruptcy square@24 force-triggers a hard check (land there under $8M -> instant bust). Most common reckless death (100% of never-fund runs).
- SCOOPED (beaten_to_market): dog reaches FIRST_TO_FILE=36 before you clear submission (open Vance / pass gate38). 'YOU CAME IN SECOND.' At d3 the deadline is turn 22 - now a LIVE threat that converges with the clock; at d5 it's turn 18 (6.4% of careful runs scooped). The dog can't be approved, so 36 = 'they filed first' = your race ends.
- RUNWAY GONE (failed_endpoint): turn > maxTurns(22) before summit. With the tighter clock and idle burn this can now actually bite a dawdler.
- CLINICAL HOLD (permanent): goodwill <= 0 AT the submission gate (reached Vance with no credibility left).

Exit-before-top via buyout = ACQUIRED. Everything routes through existing endGame()/computeScore()/rankTitle() with the funny titles intact (Regulatory Unicorn, Never Raised Series B, Scooped at the Buzzer).
scoring:
Reuse computeScore() with board-position swapped for the old value stat; FIX-7 rebalances so spread comes from things that VARY among winners. valuePct = idxToPct(S.pos) via VALUE_BANDS (0-97).

APPROVED (valuePct*7 DROPPED - it was a constant 679 for every winner since all approve at pos39; weight redistributed to varying terms):
score = 1000 + turnsLeft*25 + capitalLeft*4 + data*5 + goodwill*3 + reviewModifier*15 + difficulty*120 - integrityFlags*20 - competitorPct
  - turnsLeft*25 (was 40 - speed was over-weighted, dwarfing strategy)
  - capitalLeft*4 (was 2 - thrift now matters)
  - data*5, goodwill*3 - Vance inputs, now reachable since Vance ALWAYS fires
  - reviewModifier*15 - the LLM verdict modifier, now on the live path for 100% of winners
  - integrity*-20 (was -30 - a dirty win is penalized but not auto-bottom; FIX makes clean play viable so dirty is a real choice, not forced)

ACQUIRED (valuePct*9 KEPT - position genuinely varies here, rewards selling HIGH):
score = 400 + valuePct*9 + capitalLeft*4 + data*2 + difficulty*80 - integrityFlags*15
  Selling at $2.5B (idx~31, pct~78) ~ 1100 pts -> 'Exited Before Series B'.

LOSS (dignified-failure gradient):
score = pos*60 + valuePct*3 + data + max(0, capitalLeft)
  A scooped-at-idx35 run scores ~2100+ (dignified); an early bankruptcy scores near 0.

Efficiency bonuses are emergent: turnsLeft*25 + capitalLeft*4 mean a careful ~19%-spare turn-16 summit out-scores a cushy dawdle. integrityFlags carry a -20 tax each, so fast-and-dirty can win the climb but lose the leaderboard - and now ACTUALLY reaches the leaderboard (the draft's dirty path just lost outright by self-scooping; FIX-5 dog tuning makes dirty viable-but-taxed).
competitor:
The dog climbs the SAME snake polyline (top-track offset, scaleX(-1), existing .board-rival). It draws no cards, burns no capital - a pure clock with teeth.

ADVANCE (FIX-5 - the draft's competitor formula ceil(55/(16-d+3)) evaluated to 4 for ALL d, so difficulty did nothing, AND BURN_NORMALIZER was undefined). Replaced with an EXPLICIT square-step:
  dogStep = (1.1 + 0.18*difficulty) * char.competitorMult
  d1=1.28, d2=1.46, d3=1.64, d4=1.82, d5=2.00 squares/turn.
End of every takeTurn(): S.competitorPos += dogStep + (S.dogSurge||0); S.dogSurge=0.
Dirty Enroll draws (integrity flag) add a +1.5 SURGE that turn ('rushing wakes the competition'). An FDA draw nudges the dog -1 square that turn ('engaging the agency early buys breathing room' - the engine's competitor:-1).

SCOOP: FIRST_TO_FILE = idx36 (just below submission gate@38). If competitorPos >= 36 BEFORE the cat clears submission -> lose('beaten_to_market'), 'Scooped at the Buzzer.' Deadlines: d1 turn29, d3 turn22(=maxTurns, tight), d5 turn18 (you MUST hustle). Tension bar = competitorPos/36 as %.

INVESTOR FIX (FIX-5 - was near-unwinnable at mult 1.25 / 8% win): competitorMult nerfed 1.25 -> 1.10 (d3 Investor dogStep=1.80, deadline turn20 - tense, not doomed; sim 99.3% win). AND its upside now touches the LIVE axis instead of dead capital: Investor's '+$30 start' is kept for flavor but PLUS 'first scoop attempt fails once' (on the first time competitorPos crosses 36, reset dog to 30 and log 'a delay at the competitor's site buys you one more turn') - a real lifeline on the only fail axis that bound it.

The FDA-draw decision is now tactically real: spend a turn calming the dog (-1) and building goodwill, or risk the buzzer.

character_bonuses.lawyer_DLay: start goodwill +8, badNewsShield 0.5 (halves bad-card capital/goodwill hits; Vance refile resets readiness to need-12 not need-8)
character_bonuses.scientist_OVrsink: start data +14, cleanBonus +5 data on every R&D draw
character_bonuses.professor_Goetta: start capital +8 (=120), raiseBonus 1.6x on funding draws, first raise no goodwill hit
character_bonuses.investor_NVested: start capital +30 (=142), competitorMult 1.10 (was 1.25); FIRST scoop attempt fails once (dog resets to 30) — upside now touches the live fail axis

verified_outcomes_5000_runs_per_cfg.method: PowerShell Monte-Carlo of the exact turn loop (draw+spin same turn, idle burn, land-only funding, dogStep)
verified_outcomes_5000_runs_per_cfg.careful_d3: win 98.6% | bankrupt 1.2% | scoop 0.1% | runway 0% | cap-left ~19% | finish turn ~16
verified_outcomes_5000_runs_per_cfg.clock_now_live: winners finish turn ~16 of 22 (~6 turns slack); at d3+ dog deadline (turn 22) converges with runway — both threats real
verified_outcomes_5000_runs_per_cfg.value_target: ~15% spare for messy human play; sim's ~19% is the clean-AI ceiling
